detailed_analysis: src/agents_sync/tool_specs/codex.py is a thin Layer-3 spec-factory (per architecture.md §4 / §7) that wires the Codex IO adapters into one AgenticToolSpec. It contains a single public function, build_codex_spec, comprising six tiny local closures and one flat AgenticToolSpec(...) data literal. The file is 133 lines (well under the 300-line module limit) and the function carries no real branching (cyclomatic complexity ~1; the return statement is a nested dataclass literal, not control flow). No security smells: no hardcoded secrets, no injection vectors, no unsafe deserialization, no silent except. The secret_policy egress requirement (NFR-15 / goal 6) is correctly honoured by forwarding `config=config` into build_mcp_server_io (line 120); the non-mcp cells legitimately omit secret_policy because NFR-15 scopes detection to env/headers/auth.* fields that exist only on mcp_server artifacts. Architectural conformance is clean: the concrete tool name "codex" appears only in this adapter/factory layer (goal 5 / stable-abstractions rule), no use case is imported, and the closures conform to the ParseFn Protocol. The only substantive observations are (1) DRY: the slash_command parse/render closures are duplicated near-verbatim across codex.py, claude.py, and opencode.py — three occurrences, meeting the Rule-of-Three threshold the project already applies via _rules_factory.py and _mcp_server_factory.py for the rules and mcp_server cells; and (2) minor naming: the local closures use single-domain-word names (parse_agent, render_agent) rather than the project's two-word preference, though they match the established sibling convention and are file-local. Neither rises above SMELL/MINOR. No CRITICAL or MAJOR findings.

highest_fan_out.imports: agentic_tool_spec, tool_specs._mcp_server_factory, tool_specs._rules_factory, mcp_server_io, codex_io, markdown_yaml_metadata_block, slash_command_io

findings.problem: The slash_command parse/render closure pair (lines 57-80) is duplicated near-verbatim in claude.py (lines 51-74) and opencode.py — three occurrences differing only by the agentic_tool_name literal. The project already extracts the analogous rules and mcp_server cells via _rules_factory.build_global_rules_io and _mcp_server_factory.build_mcp_server_io; a build_slash_command_io factory would remove this repetition. Meets Fowler's Rule of Three. Non-blocking: each block is small.

findings.problem: Local closures use bare verb+single-domain-word names rather than the project's two-word-minimum preference (AGENTS.md §4). The names are unambiguous in the file-local factory context and match the sibling convention in claude.py, so impact is minimal; flagged only for completeness.

verdict_summary: Clean Layer-3 spec factory; no CRITICAL/MAJOR findings; only a cross-file DRY smell (slash_command closures, 3x) and a minor naming nit.
